event.title: I ♥ Free Software Day in Pesaro, Italy

body.p: With a slight delay, we’re celebrating our love for Free Software in Pesaro too: hurray! In the main hall of the “Guglielmo Marconi” Science High School, on Thursday 12 March at 2.00 pm, forty final-year students will take part in a session where the four freedoms of software will be explained and, whilst we’re at it, the video from the “Public Money? Public Code!”
The event is open to everyone (we’re talking about Open Source, it couldn’t be any other way) and no registration is required.
The meeting will be conducted by Giacomo Alessandroni, a FSFE volunteer and IT and Robotics teacher. The official language of the meeting is Italian.
